Title: Identification of protein stability determinants in chloroplasts

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PsbE-derived N-terminus destabilized GFP relative to PetB, PetD and PGK N-termini; PsbE C-terminus had only a moderate effect.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The native PsbE N-terminus was used as the main chloroplast reporter scaffold. 2. Changing the amino acid after initiator Met in the PsbE-GFP fusion revealed strong stability differences among amino acids in plastids. 3. The PsbE N-terminus itself conferred relatively low GFP accumulation compared with several other N-termini, indicating additional N-terminal sequence determinants beyond the penultimate residue.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transplastomic chloroplast expression; site-directed codon exchange; GFP fusion reporter assay; Western blotting; RNA blotting; polysome loading analysis; mass spectrometry for N-terminal processing in Glu variant
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn promoter with rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence for gfp reporter; constitutive plastid promoter context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse conditions; chloroplast transgene expression context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PsbE is an essential structural and functional component of photosystem II, but in this study it served as a source of N- and C-terminal peptide sequences to probe chloroplast protein stability rather than as a direct nutrient metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome b6 subunit of cytochrome b6f complex
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The PetB N-terminus conferred exceptionally high GFP stability in plastids; the C-terminus had little effect.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PetB N-terminal fusion yielded very high GFP accumulation in chloroplasts. 2. Despite sharing Ser as penultimate residue with PsbE, PetB N-terminus stabilized GFP much more strongly, demonstrating that sequence elements downstream of the penultimate residue are major stability determinants. 3. The PetB N-terminus was proposed as a useful stabilizing sequence for plastid engineering.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transplastomic chloroplast expression; GFP fusion reporter assay; Western blotting; RNA blotting; polysome loading analysis; parallel assay in Escherichia coli
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn promoter with rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence for gfp reporter; constitutive plastid promoter context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse conditions; chloroplast transgene expression context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in Discussion as a sequence conferring exceptionally high protein stability for plastid transgene design.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subunit IV of cytochrome b6f complex
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The PetD N-terminus conferred high GFP stability in plastids, whereas the C-terminus had little effect.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PetD N-terminal fusion strongly stabilized GFP in chloroplasts. 2. PetD C-terminal fusion produced similar GFP levels to most other C-terminal fusions. 3. Results support the conclusion that chloroplast protein stability is largely encoded in N-terminal sequences.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transplastomic chloroplast expression; GFP fusion reporter assay; Western blotting; RNA blotting; parallel assay in Escherichia coli
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn promoter with rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence for gfp reporter; constitutive plastid promoter context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse conditions; chloroplast transgene expression context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a terminal sequence donor rather than a directly interrogated metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The Rps12 N-terminus conferred low GFP stability in plastids and very low stability in E. coli; the C-terminus had little effect.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rps12 N-terminal fusion destabilized GFP compared with PetB, PetD and PGK N-termini. 2. This effect could not be explained solely by its penultimate residue, indicating additional N-terminal determinants. 3. Rps12 itself was suggested to be protected in its native ribosomal complex, explaining why its isolated terminus behaves destabilizingly in the reporter assay.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transplastomic chloroplast expression; GFP fusion reporter assay; Western blotting; RNA blotting; parallel assay in Escherichia coli
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn promoter with rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence for gfp reporter; constitutive plastid promoter context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse conditions; chloroplast transgene expression context
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Ribosomal small subunit proteins (complex context, not individually tested)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient-metabolism gene; included because its terminal peptide sequence was directly assayed as a protein stability determinant.

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stroma (inferred for plastid methionine aminopeptidase activity)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to be a major determinant of plastid protein stability by removing initiator Met and thereby exposing the penultimate residue that influences protein half-life.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The study proposes methionine aminopeptidase action as one of three main determinants of plastid protein stability. 2. Inefficient Met excision was experimentally supported for the Glu reporter variant by mass spectrometry. 3. Stability differences among reporter proteins are partly explained by whether initiator Met is removed or retained.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inference from transplastomic reporter series; GFP purification; mass spectrometric N-terminal peptide analysis of Glu variant
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This processing enzyme is central to the mechanistic interpretation, but no individual plastid methionine aminopeptidase gene was cloned or genetically manipulated in this paper.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Nuclear-encoded plastid-targeted plant protein discussed from literature
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly regulated; declines when ammonium is sufficient
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Discussed as a possible physiological substrate of an N-terminus-dependent plastid protein degradation pathway because it carries Cys at the N-terminus after transit peptide processing.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nitrite reductase was the only imported chloroplast protein in a cited dataset with a strongly destabilizing N-terminal Cys. 2. The paper proposes it as a potential endogenous substrate of the plastid N-end-rule-like pathway. 3. This was not directly tested experimentally in the present study.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ammonium sufficiency mentioned from prior literature
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background candidate substrate mentioned in Discussion; not a core experimentally tested gene in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a phosphate group from ATP to 3-phosphoglycerate to form 1,3-bisphosphoglycerate in the Calvin cycle direction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Background enzyme used as source of terminal peptide sequence; not shown to causally alter a nutrient metabolite in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Green leaf chloroplast context (inferred from chloroplast protein source sequences)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PGK N-terminus conferred high stability to GFP in plastids and E. coli; PGK C-terminus had only a moderate destabilizing effect compared with other C-termini.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The N-terminal nine amino acids of PGK strongly stabilized GFP in chloroplasts. 2. The C-terminal eight amino acids of PGK had only a modest effect on GFP accumulation. 3. PGK itself was not functionally dissected for metabolite accumulation; it served as a source sequence to probe chloroplast protein stability determinants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transplastomic chloroplast expression; GFP fusion reporter assay; Western blotting; RNA blotting; polysome loading analysis; parallel assay in Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Prrn promoter with rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence for gfp reporter; constitutive plastid promoter context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard greenhouse conditions; chloroplast transgene expression context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PGK-derived N-terminal sequence could be used to improve stability of recombinant proteins expressed from plastid genomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PGK is a metabolic enzyme, it is not a core nutrient-metabolism causal gene in this paper; only its terminal peptide sequences were tested as stability determinants.
